notes: All Windows tools must be downloaded from the official source by the investigator. This manifest never causes binaries to be committed to the repository. Where 'install_method' is 'manual_download', the build script will skip the file and only create the destination folder.

tools.name: Autopsy
tools.purpose: Open-source GUI digital forensics platform built on The Sleuth Kit. Used for image analysis, keyword search, timeline, file carving.
tools.notes: Install on the analyst workstation, not the suspect host. Carry the installer on USB for offline field analysis.

tools.name: Chainsaw
tools.purpose: Sigma-based fast event-log searching and detection across collected EVTX files.

tools.name: Eric Zimmerman's EZ Tools
tools.purpose: Suite of open-source CLI parsers (MFTECmd, RECmd, PECmd, EvtxECmd, AmcacheParser, JLECmd, LECmd, SBECmd, etc.) for Windows artifact analysis.
tools.notes: Used widely in DFIR; supports cross-validation against KAPE output.

tools.name: FTK Imager
tools.purpose: Create forensic images and verified copies; preview FAT/NTFS/EXT/HFS/APFS volumes.
tools.notes: Registration may be required. Use only with proper authority and a hardware write-blocker for raw imaging.

tools.name: Hayabusa
tools.purpose: Fast Windows EVTX threat-hunting and triage tool with Sigma rule support.
tools.notes: Run against EVTX exports from the live-response collector.

tools.name: KAPE
tools.purpose: Modular Windows triage collection and processing of forensic artifacts (Kroll Artifact Parser and Extractor).
tools.notes: Each investigator must register and download. Read Kroll's licensing terms before commercial use.

tools.name: MemProcFS
tools.purpose: Mounts a memory image as a virtual filesystem for fast triage.
tools.notes: Pair with WinPmem or other acquisition tool.

tools.name: Plaso
tools.purpose: log2timeline / Plaso — extract events from files, directories, images, and devices to build super-timelines.
tools.notes: Outputs `.plaso` storage files; analyze with `psort.py` or import to Timesketch.

tools.name: Sysinternals Suite
tools.purpose: Autoruns, Process Explorer, Handle, Sigcheck, TCPView, etc. — read-mostly diagnostic utilities.
tools.notes: Prefer running from the USB; do not install on the suspect host.

tools.name: Velociraptor
tools.purpose: Open-source endpoint monitoring and DFIR; build a single-binary offline collector for targeted artifact collection.
tools.notes: Build the offline collector configuration on your analyst workstation, then copy the resulting collector binary onto the triage USB.

tools.name: Volatility 3
tools.notes: Run analysis on the analyst workstation against acquired memory images.

tools.name: WinPmem
tools.purpose: Acquire physical memory from a live Windows host.
tools.notes: Requires Administrator. Memory acquisition modifies a small amount of system state — document it. Use only with authority.
